perturbation.original_text: Upon written notice from DISCLOSING PARTY at any time, COMPANY shall return to DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies or derivative works thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon written notice from DISCLOSING PARTY, provided such notice is given within 30 days of termination of discussions, COMPANY shall return to DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies or derivative works thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting the time frame in which DISCLOSING PARTY can request the return or destruction of confidential information. The original clause allowed DISCLOSING PARTY to request this 'at any time', but the modified version restricts this right to within 30 days of termination. If the termination of discussions occurs in the future, the 'at any time' conflicts with 'within 30 days of termination', creating ambiguity and a contradiction within the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the property of DISCLOSING PARTY. This Agreement does not grant any rights to COMPANY with respect to the Confidential Information. Upon written notice from DISCLOSING PARTY, provided such notice is given within 30 days of termination of discussions, COMPANY shall return to DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy all Confidential Information and any copies or derivative works thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original clause allowed DISCLOSING PARTY to request the return of confidential information 'at any time'. The modified version restricts this right to within 30 days of termination of discussions. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. Therefore, this creates a clear inconsistency and ambiguity because it attempts to limit the scope of a previously unlimited timeframe. The phrase 'at any time' strongly conflicts with 'within 30 days of termination', which constitutes a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to Confidential Information and no modification or waiver of the terms of this Agreement shall be binding unless it is in writing signed by an authorized representative of each party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to Confidential Information and no modification or waiver of the terms of this Agreement shall be binding unless it is in writing signed by an authorized representative of each party. However, emailed confirmations from DISCLOSING PARTY will be considered as acceptance of changes.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause states that modifications require a written document signed by both parties. The added sentence introduces an exception: Email confirmations from DISCLOSING PARTY are sufficient for acceptance of changes. This contradicts the previous sentence, creating uncertainty regarding the requirements for modifying the agreement and creating a loophole for unapproved changes. The conflict lies directly between the formal requirement and the informal exception.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to Confidential Information and no modification or waiver of the terms of this Agreement shall be binding unless it is in writing signed by an authorized representative of each party. However, emailed confirmations from DISCLOSING PARTY will be considered as acceptance of changes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that email confirmations from the DISCLOSING PARTY will be considered as acceptance of changes. This directly contradicts the original clause, which states that all modifications must be in writing and signed by both parties. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, but the altered portion introduces a clear and irreconcilable contradiction regarding the process for modifying the agreement, because it establishes two distinct paths for changes to be accepted, thus the changed text strongly contradicts the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information is provided “As is” without any warranties, express, implied or otherwise, regarding its accuracy and completeness.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information is provided “As is” without any warranties, express, implied or otherwise, regarding its accuracy and completeness. However, DISCLOSING PARTY warrants that it has the right to disclose the information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause explicitly disclaims any warranties regarding the accuracy and completeness of the confidential information. The added sentence introduces a warranty: DISCLOSING PARTY warrants that it has the right to disclose the information. This conflicts with the 'As is' provision by creating a direct warranty regarding the right to disclose, contradicting the initial disclaimer of all warranties. This is a blatant disregard for what has been previously stated in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information is provided “As is” without any warranties, express, implied or otherwise, regarding its accuracy and completeness. However, DISCLOSING PARTY warrants that it has the right to disclose the information. Any representations regarding the Confidential Information, if any, will be contained in the documentation consummating the business opportunity, if any.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text and the contradicted text both state that all confidential information is provided 'As is' without any warranties, express, implied, or otherwise, regarding its accuracy and completeness. However, the changed text adds 'However, DISCLOSING PARTY warrants that it has the right to disclose the information.' This addition directly contradicts the 'As is' provision, as it introduces a specific warranty (right to disclose) while the original clause disclaims all warranties. The contradiction is in-text because the changed text is also present in the contradicted section. This clearly aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: COMPANY agrees to cooperate with DISCLOSING PARTY if DISCLOSING PARTY elects to seek reasonable protective arrangements or oppose such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: COMPANY agrees to cooperate with DISCLOSING PARTY if DISCLOSING PARTY elects to seek reasonable protective arrangements or oppose such disclosure, unless such cooperation would incur significant legal expenses for COMPANY.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause states that the COMPANY agrees to cooperate. The added text creates an exception where such cooperation incurs significant legal expenses, creating an ambiguity if such cooperation applies. The addition of the condition directly contradicts the obligation previously established, since significant legal expenses have a broad interpretation that can be exploited, opening the possibility for legal disputation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPANY shall provide notice to DISCLOSING PARTY of any demand made upon it under lawful process to disclose or provide the Confidential Information. COMPANY agrees to cooperate with DISCLOSING PARTY if DISCLOSING PARTY elects to seek reasonable protective arrangements or oppose such disclosure, unless such cooperation would incur significant legal expenses for COMPANY.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the COMPANY's obligation to cooperate with the DISCLOSING PARTY, stating that cooperation is not required if it incurs significant legal expenses. This directly contradicts the initial agreement to cooperate, creating an ambiguity. The original text stated COMPANY 'agrees to cooperate', while the added text creates an exception if such cooperation incurs significant legal expenses, creating an ambiguity and thus contradiction, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation.
